scope: v0.15 km-bench: 1km Canaries training-grid feasibility on RTX 5090 (32GB)
method: Measured warmed step throughput + peak VRAM at 7 grid sizes (10.5k-168k cols, spatial-tile cost proxies of the real d02 case); core=dycore+rad+PBL-MYNN, boundary/GWD/NoahMP OFF. Calibrated to measured full-production 128x128 step (178 ms/step @dt18 -> 35.6 s/fc-hr, perf_fix_findings.json) via additive/multiplicative carries -> speedup band. CPU denom=28-rank gfortran dmpar, linear in cells (43.28 s/fc-hr @128^2, 61.03 @150^2). All anchors measured on this workstation.

peak_vram.note: LINEAR in ncol -> radiation/MYNN 16384-col tiling does NOT cap total VRAM; a full-grid un-chunked intermediate (~19-28 GiB single op at target) is the real ceiling. Measured: 167904 cols ran @26.25 GiB; 209880 cols OOM (28.44 GiB single alloc).

island_answer: 560x280 SMALL target already spans the WHOLE 7-island archipelago at 1km and FITS (~24.5 GiB). 640x320/720x360 are denser all-islands footprints that do NOT fit. All 7 islands (TF GC LP VF LR LG EH) fit in ONE 1km grid at the 560x280 footprint; per-island splitting only needed for a >600x300 footprint.
saturation_hypothesis: REFUTED. ms/step ~LINEAR in ncol (slope ~4.9-5.0 ms/1000col; throughput plateaus ~195-200 cols/ms then declines). GPU does NOT saturate better at large grids; per-cell cost slightly WORSENS. The 6-10x per-cell roofline did not hold for the full coupled step (compute/bandwidth-bound at large ncol; launch-bound only via the fixed ~20ms intercept at small grids).
speedup_explained: Speedup rises ~1.1x (128^2) -> ~1.5-2.7x at 1km targets ONLY because the fixed ~20ms GPU launch intercept amortizes over more cells, NOT per-cell saturation. dt=18s assumed both sides; true 1km CFL dt~6s -> 3x steps both -> same ratio, 3x longer walls.
bottom_line: GPU rewrite is USEFUL but MODEST at 1km scale on the 5090: ~1.5-2.7x faster than 28-rank CPU-WRF (centered ~2x), ~1.9-2.7x with S2 megakernel. 560x280 FITS (24.5 GiB); 640x320 & 720x360 do NOT (32/41 GiB); cap ~600x300. NOT the 6-10x the saturation hypothesis predicted.
